---
title: "testing varabale explorer"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-08"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(tidyverse)
library(tidycensus)
library(labelled)
library(tidyr)
census_api_key("b8f6c712cbbb9d5a172c960ee27f3dc192408eda", install = TRUE, overwrite=TRUE)

years <- c(2013, 2014, 2015)

acs_5yr_by_region_wide <- map_dfr(years, \(yr) {
  get_acs(
      survey = "acs5",
      geography = "region",
      output = "wide",
    variables = c(
        raceBlackOne_DP = "DP05_0038",
        raceBlackOne_SexByAge = "B01001B_001"
    ),
    year = yr
  ) |>
    mutate(year = yr)
})

var_label(acs_5yr_by_region_wide) <- list(
  raceBlackOne_DPE = "RACE > Total population > One race > Black or African American [DP05_0038E]",
  raceBlackOne_SexByAgeE = "Sex › by Age › ( Black or African American Alone) [B01001B_001E]"
)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
